content.full_text:
Phone (209) 533-7755
RoGER A. BROWN
I:wyer
38 North Washington Street
Post Offrce Box 475
Sonora, California 95370
June 10, 1992
Fax (209) 533-7757
i'
Terinical Assistance Consultant 
-
Fair Political Practices Commission 
=
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
RE: Advice Request
Dear Wayne:
This is to request advice on behalf of one of my clients concerning the lobbying provisions of
the political Reform Act. My client is eager to comply with the law and intends to rely upon your
advice in deciding the appropriate action to take.
My client is a firm specializing in research and development of media stntegies intended to
influence iegislative and executive policy decisions in the State of Califomia. We need your advice
whether the activities described herein would qualify my client as a lobbyist or lobbying firm with
filing responsibilities under the Act.
My client will be hired to conduct news conferences, among other things. You may assume
they will ieceive over $2,0@ in compensation in a month to do the things described herein. They
may be hired by one or more members of the legislature or by a charitable non-profit corporation or
a for-profit corporation.
As part of their effort to organize news conferences, my client will directly contact
legislators, legislative staff and certain administrative officials to coordinate their participation in the
news conferences. They may also advise members and their staff on media strategy.
The principal purpose of the media strategies contemplated is to mobilize public opinion in
support of or opposition to various legislative proposals. There will be no direct contact with any
legislative or administrative official for the purpose of influencing their decisions on legislation. All
direct contacts are for the purpose of devising and implementing a media strategy to get the public
to act.
i-€
:IG
r'J


Wayne Imberi
Technical Assistance Consultant
RE: Advice Reouest
June 10, 1992
Page2
Under the facts and circumstances described above, will my client qualify as a lobbyist or
lobbying firm with registration and reporting responsibilities under the Political Reform Act?
Very truly yours,
G"*fur<
Ro8er \ Brown
RAB/smj:333s.05

sections.question: Under the facts and circumstances described above, will my client qualify as a lobbyist or lobbying firm with registration and reporting responsibilities under the Political Reform Act?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a media strategy firm that conducts news conferences and coordinates with legislators and staff to mobilize public opinion on legislative proposals, while receiving over $2,000 monthly compensation, qualifies as a lobbyist or lobbying firm with registration and reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/letter seeking advice, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion or advice section present in document. Question is clearly stated. Document appears to be the first page of correspondence to FPPC; FPPC response letter not included in this extract.

embedding.summary: Incoming request from attorney Roger A. Brown seeking FPPC advice on behalf of a client regarding whether their media strategy and news conference activities constitute lobbying activities requiring registration under the Political Reform Act.
